title: Endpoint Security and Device Hardening Policy
content:
Endpoint Security and Device Hardening Policy v2.5 — Aligned with NIST SP 800-123, CIS Benchmarks, and DoD STIG Endpoint guidelines.

1. APPROVED SOFTWARE AND INSTALLATION
1.1 Only IT-approved software from the enterprise software catalog may be installed on corporate devices.
1.2 Software requests must be submitted through the IT Service Management portal and approved by the user's manager and IT Security.
1.3 Installation of unapproved, unlicensed, pirated, or cracked software is strictly prohibited and constitutes a policy violation subject to disciplinary action.
1.4 Users must not install software from untrusted sources, peer-to-peer networks, or unverified websites.
1.5 All software must be obtained from approved repositories: enterprise software portal, vendor-official download pages, or approved package managers (e.g., Chocolatey enterprise feed, SCCM Software Center).
1.6 Open-source software must be reviewed and approved by IT Security before installation. The review includes license compliance, vulnerability scan, and supply chain risk assessment.
1.7 Browser extensions must be limited to the enterprise-approved whitelist managed via Group Policy.

2. OPERATING SYSTEM HARDENING
2.1 All endpoints must run a supported operating system version receiving active security updates.
2.2 End-of-life operating systems (e.g., Windows 10 after Oct 2025, macOS versions older than N-2) must be upgraded within 90 days of EOL or isolated from the network.
2.3 Endpoints must be hardened according to the applicable CIS Benchmark (Level 1 minimum, Level 2 for systems handling sensitive data).
2.4 Unnecessary services, ports, and protocols must be disabled.
2.5 Local administrator accounts must be managed via LAPS (Local Administrator Password Solution) with passwords rotated every 30 days.
2.6 AutoRun and AutoPlay must be disabled on all endpoints.
2.7 Full disk encryption (BitLocker for Windows, FileVault for macOS) must be enabled with recovery keys escrowed in Active Directory or MDM.

3. ANTIVIRUS AND ENDPOINT DETECTION
3.1 All endpoints must have the enterprise-approved EDR (Endpoint Detection and Response) agent installed and actively running.
3.2 Disabling, uninstalling, or tampering with the EDR agent is prohibited and will trigger an automated security alert.
3.3 Real-time protection, behavior monitoring, and cloud-delivered protection must be enabled.
3.4 Antivirus definitions must be updated at least every 4 hours.
3.5 Full system scans must be performed weekly.
3.6 Detected threats must be automatically quarantined and reported to the SOC.

4. PATCH MANAGEMENT
4.1 Critical and High severity patches (CVSS 7.0+) must be applied within 14 days of release.
4.2 Critical zero-day patches with active exploitation must be applied within 48 hours.
4.3 All other patches must be applied within 30 days of release.
4.4 Patch compliance must be monitored continuously. Devices below 95% compliance must be flagged for remediation.
4.5 Patches must be tested in a staging environment before enterprise-wide deployment.
4.6 Exceptions to patching timelines require a documented risk acceptance signed by the system owner and CISO.

5. REMOVABLE MEDIA
5.1 Use of unauthorized USB storage devices is prohibited on all corporate endpoints.
5.2 Approved encrypted USB devices may be used with IT Security authorization for specific business needs.
5.3 USB device control policies must be enforced via endpoint management tools (Intune, SCCM, or equivalent).
5.4 CD/DVD autorun must be disabled enterprise-wide.

6. MOBILE DEVICE MANAGEMENT
6.1 Corporate mobile devices and BYOD devices accessing enterprise resources must be enrolled in the enterprise MDM solution.
6.2 MDM policies must enforce: device encryption, screen lock (6-digit PIN minimum or biometric), remote wipe capability, and jailbreak/root detection.
6.3 Enterprise applications on mobile devices must use app-level encryption and containerization.
6.4 Devices not compliant with MDM policy must be blocked from accessing corporate email and applications within 24 hours.

7. REFERENCES
- NIST SP 800-123 Guide to General Server Security
- NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 CM-7 Least Functionality
- CIS Benchmarks for Windows 11, macOS, Ubuntu
- DoD STIG Windows 11 V1R4
- CIS Controls v8 Controls 2.1-2.7, 4.1-4.12, 10.1-10.7
